terms.definition: Python's built-in deterministic function-level CPU profiler implemented in C; traces every function call.
Tool that measures where a program spends its time (CPU) or allocates its memory; deterministic (every call) or sampling (statistical).
Profiler that records every function call (like cProfile); accurate but slows the program.
Profiler that periodically samples the call stack (like py-spy); low overhead, less precise on rare calls.
Total time spent inside a function itself, excluding sub-calls; the metric for finding where the actual work happens.
Cumulative time spent in a function including all sub-calls; the metric for finding the slow path.
Standard-library module for sorting, filtering and printing cProfile output (`pstats.Stats(...).sort_stats(...)`).
Browser-based icicle-chart visualizer for cProfile output; pip-installable.
Low-overhead sampling profiler for Python; attaches to a running process without code changes; production-safe.
Line-level profiler that times each line of decorated functions; better signal than cProfile for narrow optimization.
Visualization where width corresponds to time and depth corresponds to call stack; lets you spot bottlenecks at a glance.
